_comment: Hand-curated 20-question smoke fixture for LongMemEval Phase 1 CI runs. Stratified across 5 categories: 5 single-session-user, 4 multi-session-user, 4 temporal-reasoning-ability, 3 knowledge-update, 4 single-session-assistant. Questions are synthetic but representative of the LongMemEval paper's task distribution. Replace with real dataset questions when HUGGINGFACE_TOKEN is available.

questions.question: What programming language did the user say they prefer for backend development?

session_history.content: Hi, I need some help setting up a new project.
Sure, I'd be happy to help! What kind of project are you working on?
It's a REST API for a task management app. I prefer Python for backend development — it's what I know best.
Great choice! Python has excellent libraries for building REST APIs. Are you thinking FastAPI, Django, or Flask?
Probably FastAPI since I've heard it's fast and has automatic docs.
FastAPI is a solid choice. It's built on Starlette and Pydantic, supports async natively, and generates OpenAPI docs automatically.

questions.question: What is the name of the user's cat?

session_history.content: Can you help me write a poem for my cat's birthday?
Of course! What's your cat's name?
Her name is Mochi. She's a 3-year-old calico.

What a sweet name! Here's a birthday poem for Mochi:

Soft-pawed Mochi, three years old,
Calico colors, bright and bold...
That's perfect, thank you! She'll love it.

questions.question: What city does the user live in?

session_history.content: What are some good coffee shops near me? I'm in Austin, Texas.
Austin has a vibrant coffee scene! Some popular spots include Houndstooth Coffee, Flat Track Coffee, and Medici.
I've been to Houndstooth — it's great. Any newer spots?
Sure! Merit Coffee and Dominican Joe are also well-regarded in Austin.

questions.question: What marathon time is the user training for?

session_history.content: I'm training for a marathon and need help with a training plan.
Happy to help! What's your goal finish time and current weekly mileage?
I want to finish under 4 hours. I'm currently running about 25 miles a week.
Great goal! For a sub-4-hour marathon, you'll want to build to about 40-50 miles per week over 16-20 weeks.

questions.question: What is the user's job title?

session_history.content: I need help writing a performance review self-evaluation.
I can help with that. What's your role and what are some highlights from this year?
I'm a senior product manager. This year I launched two major features, improved NPS by 12 points, and onboarded a new engineering team.

Those are strong achievements. Here's a draft self-evaluation opening:

'As a Senior Product Manager, I drove significant customer value this year...'

questions.question: What is the user's daughter's name?

session_history.content: I need help picking a birthday present for my daughter.
How old is she and what are her interests?
She's turning 7. Her name is Lily and she loves dinosaurs and drawing.
A dinosaur art set or a dinosaur activity book could be perfect for Lily!
Perfect. Can you help me write a birthday card too?
Of course! Here's one: 'Happy Birthday, Lily! Seven is a magical number — just like you!'

questions.question: What allergy did the user mention?

session_history.content: Can you suggest some recipes for dinner tonight?
Sure! Any dietary restrictions I should know about?
Yes, I have a gluten intolerance, so everything needs to be gluten-free.
Got it. Here are some gluten-free dinner ideas: grilled salmon with roasted vegetables, chicken stir-fry with rice noodles, or a bean and vegetable curry.
The salmon sounds great. What sides would go well with it?
Roasted asparagus, quinoa, or a simple salad are all gluten-free and pair well with salmon.

questions.question: What company is the user applying to?

session_history.content: I'm applying for a job and need help preparing for interviews.
I'd love to help! Which company and role are you interviewing for?
I'm applying to Stripe for a staff software engineer position.
Stripe has a rigorous technical interview process. For a staff engineer role, expect system design, coding, and leadership discussions.
Any tips for the system design round specifically?
For Stripe's system design round, focus on distributed systems, idempotency (critical for payments), and scalability at high transaction volumes.

questions.question: What instrument does the user play?

session_history.content: I've been playing guitar for about 5 years and want to level up my skills.
That's great! What genre do you primarily play, and what do you feel is holding you back?
I mostly play acoustic fingerstyle. I struggle with thumb independence.
Thumb independence is a classic challenge for fingerstyle players. Try practicing patterns like P-IMI where the thumb maintains a constant bass pattern while fingers play melodies.

questions.question: What did the user say about their project deadline in the earlier conversation?

session_history.content: Quick question — we talked about my project timeline a few weeks ago. Our deadline is June 15th, right?
Yes, based on what you shared, your project deadline is June 15th.
Okay good. We're a bit behind but I think we can still make it.
What's the current status? Is there anything specific blocking progress?
The API integration is taking longer than expected. We originally planned 2 weeks but it's looking more like 3.
To hit June 15th, you might need to parallelize the integration work with the frontend development. Have you considered bringing in extra help?

questions.question: When did the user say they started learning Spanish?

session_history.content: I've been learning Spanish for about three years now and I'm at an intermediate level.
Three years is solid progress! What's your current biggest challenge with Spanish?
Understanding native speakers when they talk fast. My reading is fine but listening is hard.
That's a very common intermediate plateau. Try watching Spanish TV shows with Spanish subtitles — not English. Shows like 'La Casa de Papel' or news programs work well.

questions.question: When did the user's gym membership expire?

session_history.content: I need to get back into working out. My gym membership expired last month and I haven't renewed it yet.
Getting back into a routine is great! Are you thinking about rejoining the gym or trying something different?
Maybe I'll try home workouts first to see if I can stay consistent before paying for a gym again.
That's a practical approach. Bodyweight training can be very effective — apps like Nike Training Club offer free structured workouts.

questions.question: How long ago did the user mention moving to their new apartment?

session_history.content: I moved into my new apartment two weeks ago and I'm still getting settled.
Congrats on the new place! Is there anything you need help with to get organized?
I need some help figuring out how to arrange the furniture in a small living room.
For small living rooms, the key is keeping traffic flow clear and using multi-functional furniture. A sofa with storage is great, and placing it against the wall maximizes floor space.

questions.question: What is the user's updated email address?

session_history.content: Hey, just wanted to let you know I changed my work email. The old one was alex@oldcompany.com.
Thanks for letting me know! What's the new address?
It's alex.new@company.com. Please use that going forward.
Got it, I'll use alex.new@company.com from now on.

questions.question: What is the user's new job title after the promotion?

session_history.content: I got promoted! I'm now an engineering manager instead of a senior engineer.
Congratulations! That's a big step. How are you feeling about the transition from IC to manager?
Excited but nervous. Managing people is very different from writing code.
That's a natural feeling. Focus on 1:1s, removing blockers for your team, and resisting the urge to dive into every technical problem yourself.

questions.question: What operating system is the user now using after switching?

session_history.content: I finally made the switch — I've been on Windows my whole career but I just moved to Linux full-time.
Welcome to Linux! Which distribution did you go with?
Ubuntu 24.04 LTS. Still learning the terminal shortcuts.
Ubuntu LTS is a great stable choice for getting started. For terminal shortcuts, learning tmux early will save you a lot of time.

questions.question: What recommendation did the assistant make for managing the user's code reviews?

session_history.content: My code reviews are taking too long and I keep missing things. Any advice?
A few things help: First, use a checklist — decide ahead of time what you always check (security, tests, naming, error handling) and work through it systematically rather than freeform scanning.
That's actually a great idea. What should be on the checklist?
Standard items: correctness and edge cases, test coverage, error handling, naming clarity, security (especially for user inputs), and performance for hot paths.

questions.question: What book did the assistant recommend for learning system design?

session_history.content: Can you recommend a good book for learning system design for software engineers?
The most widely recommended book is 'Designing Data-Intensive Applications' by Martin Kleppmann. It covers distributed systems, databases, and scalability in depth with practical examples.
Is it beginner-friendly or do you need a lot of prior knowledge?
It assumes some programming experience but explains concepts clearly. Most engineers with 1-2 years of experience can follow it.

questions.question: What did the assistant say the safe internal temperature for chicken should be?

session_history.content: What temperature should chicken be cooked to?
Chicken should reach an internal temperature of 165°F (74°C) to be safe to eat. Use a meat thermometer in the thickest part of the meat, avoiding the bone.
What about chicken thighs? They seem to stay pink even when cooked through.
Chicken thighs can stay pink even at 165°F due to myoglobin. If the thermometer reads 165°F, it's safe regardless of color.

questions.question: What did the assistant recommend as the first step when debugging a slow database query?

session_history.content: My PostgreSQL query is running really slow. Where do I start debugging?

The first step is to check the query execution plan using EXPLAIN ANALYZE. This shows you exactly how PostgreSQL is executing the query — whether it's doing a full table scan, using indexes, doing hash joins, etc.
I ran EXPLAIN ANALYZE and it shows a Seq Scan on a large table.
A sequential scan on a large table is usually the culprit. Add an index on the columns in your WHERE clause. Run CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY to avoid locking the table.
